Huadian Power International Corporation Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Huadian Power International Corporation Limited reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was CNY 126,012.59 million compared to CNY 141,502.22 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 126,012.59 million compared to CNY 141,502.22 million a year ago.

Net income was CNY 6,070.32 million compared to CNY 5,987.23 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.49 compared to CNY 0.46 a year ago.

Mitsubishi Electric, Toshiba, Rohm Reportedly Begin Merger Talks for Power Semiconductor Device Businesses
data.description:
Mitsubishi Electric Corporation (TSE:6503), industrial conglomerate Toshiba Corporation and chipmaker ROHM Co., Ltd. (TSE:6963) will begin negotiations to merge their power semiconductor device businesses, sources familiar with the matter said. The talks come as the Japanese government pushes for greater recognition of, and market share for, Japanese companies in the chip industry amid declining demand for electric vehicles and the growing dominance of Chinese firms. Power chips can be utilized in a wide range of applications, from electric infrastructure to servers for data centers needed for artificial intelligence. Toshiba and Rohm have already begun merger discussions, with Mitsubishi Electric joining the fray.

Denso Corp., a major auto parts maker, has been considering a takeover of Rohm.

Meituan Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Meituan reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was CNY 364,854.75 million compared to CNY 337,591.58 million a year ago. Net loss was CNY 23,355.02 million compared to net income of CNY 35,807.18 million a year ago.

Basic loss per share from continuing operations was CNY 3.84 compared to basic earnings per share from continuing operations of CNY 5.85 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was CNY 3.92 compared to diluted earnings per share from continuing operations of CNY 5.66 a year ago.

data.title: Innovent Biologics, Inc. Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Innovent Biologics, Inc. reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was CNY 13,041.52 million compared to CNY 9,421.89 million a year ago. Net income was CNY 813.57 million compared to net loss of CNY 94.63 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.48 compared to basic loss per share from continuing operations of CNY 0.06 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.47 compared to diluted loss per share from continuing operations of CNY 0.06 a year ago.

Central U.S. Factory Activity Continued To Grow in March -- Kansas City Fed
data.description:
By Jessica Coacci

Manufacturing activity in the central U.S. continued to grow in March, as the month over month indexes were all positive except for new orders for exports.

The Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City said Thursday that its Tenth District Manufacturing Survey's composite index rose to 11 in March, from 5 in February. The zero-level marks the dividing point between expansion and contraction in the sector.

"Regional manufacturing activity rose moderately from last month, posting its highest reading since July 2022," said Cortney Cowley, assistant vice president at the Kansas City Fed.

The volume of shipments and new orders indexes increased. The employment index rebounded into positive territory.

Most year-over-year indexes were positive except for new orders for exports, number of employees and finished goods inventory, the survey said.

Meanwhile, expectations for future activity grew further as expectations for production ticked up.

"Most firms still expect higher demand this year relative to 2025," Cowley added. "However, while some firms expected higher profit margins in coming months, a slightly larger share expected lower profit margins due to cost pressures," she said.

Raw materials price growth cooled slightly but continued to outpace finished product price growth, the Kansas City Fed added.

The manufacturing survey covers activity in the Tenth District, which includes Colorado, Oklahoma, Wyoming, Nebraska, Kansas, the northern half of New Mexico and the western third of Missouri.

Write to Jessica Coacci at jessica.coacci@wsj.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-26-26 1141ET
